vendors.name: ServCraft
vendors.tagline: SA-built job management for installation, repair & maintenance trades
vendors.market_position: The South African-native flagship — quotes, job cards, scheduling and invoicing, built and supported locally, with industry editions that explicitly target plumbing, electrical and the booming solar-installation trade.
pros: Genuinely SA-native ("Made in SA") with local support
Job cards + quotes + invoicing in one
cons: Smaller ecosystem than the global platforms
vendors.typical_pricing: Published: R349/user/mo (Standard); custom for 20+ users; 14-day trial.
vendors.za_specific_note: The local-champion pick — and one of the few platforms anywhere marketing directly to solar installers, the trade the load-shedding era built. Local support in SA hours is a real advantage.

vendors.name: Tradify (ZA)
vendors.tagline: All-in-one trades app with dedicated SA pricing
vendors.market_position: The strongest global player with a real South African footprint — a dedicated ZA site with full Rand pricing. Quotes, invoicing, scheduling and timesheets for 1–20 staff trade businesses.
pros: Dedicated ZA site with published ZAR pricing
Polished, easy all-in-one for small crews
cons: Per-user pricing adds up as you grow
vendors.typical_pricing: Published: Lite R480 / Pro R520 / Plus R630 per user/mo; website add-on R190/mo.
vendors.za_specific_note: Full Rand price list on a dedicated ZA page — rare transparency in this market. A safe pick for small crews that want polish over local specificity.

vendors.name: ServiceM8 (ZA)
vendors.tagline: Job management with job-based (not per-user) ZAR pricing
vendors.market_position: Global trades platform with a dedicated ZA site and Rand pricing. Its job-based pricing — unlimited users, pay per job volume — suits SA businesses with many field staff and tight margins.
pros: Free tier (up to 30 jobs/month) to start
Dedicated ZA site with ZAR pricing
Strong mobile app, signatures, card payments
vendors.typical_pricing: Published: Free (30 jobs/mo) / Starter R295 / Growing R845 / Premium R1,595 / Premium Plus R3,695 per month.
vendors.za_specific_note: The free 30-jobs tier is the cheapest legitimate way for a one-person SA trade business to start with real software — and unlimited users helps crews where per-user pricing stings.

vendors.name: Eworks Manager
vendors.tagline: Job card management with full offline mode
vendors.market_position: UK-origin field-service platform with a real South African operation (.co.za site and SA phone support). Job cards, quoting, planning and invoicing — with a full offline mode that suits SA connectivity realities.
pros: Full offline mode — works without signal
Broad feature set incl. CMMS/asset work
cons: UK-origin product, SA is a branch market
Pricing shown in GBP bands (ZAR toggle on site)
Interface less modern than newer rivals
Team tiers can overshoot small crews
vendors.typical_pricing: Team-based tiers (ZAR toggle on the pricing page); 14-day trial.
vendors.za_specific_note: The offline mode matters in SA — load-shedding may be suspended, but site connectivity isn't guaranteed. Worth shortlisting for field-heavy crews in patchy-coverage areas.

vendors.name: Field Service Cloud
vendors.tagline: SA-built job cards, fleet tracking & route optimisation
vendors.market_position: Pretoria-built field-service platform "born from the construction industry" — job cards, quoting and invoicing plus fleet/GPS tracking and route optimisation, aimed at plumbing, electrical and construction businesses.
pros: Fleet/GPS tracking + route optimisation built in
cons: Smaller and less-known than the majors
vendors.za_specific_note: The built-in vehicle tracking is unusually relevant in SA, where fleet security and fuel cost control are everyday concerns for trades businesses.

vendors.name: Odyssey Job Cards
vendors.tagline: Simple SA job cards, quotes & invoicing with mobile sign-off
vendors.market_position: Bloemfontein-built job-card system — quotations, invoices and a mobile app with photos, parts and customer sign-off. A simple, affordable, flat-priced local option for small teams.
pros: Mobile app with photos + customer sign-off
Covers the core job-card workflow well
cons: Basic next to the bigger platforms
vendors.typical_pricing: Published: R483/mo (1 admin + 5 techs); R800/mo Enterprise (3 admins + 20 techs).
vendors.za_specific_note: At R483/month flat for a 6-person team, this is among the cheapest real job-card software available to SA trades — honest value for simple needs.

vendors.name: OpenSolar
vendors.tagline: Free solar design, proposals & CRM for installers
vendors.market_position: A free, global end-to-end solar platform — design, proposals, CRM and e-signatures — used by 28,000+ installers worldwide. With SA's rooftop-solar boom creating thousands of installer businesses, a zero-cost professional toolset is compelling.
cons: No SA office — global product, no local support
Monetised via partner ecosystem (mind the nudges)
vendors.za_specific_note: No verified SA office — it works anywhere, but support is global. Pair it with a job-management tool (ServCraft, Tradify) for the business side; OpenSolar handles design and proposals.

vendors.name: Kandua
vendors.tagline: SA’s leading home-services marketplace — Santam-owned
vendors.market_position: South Africa's biggest home-services marketplace: 40,000+ vetted, background-checked pros connected to roughly R50 million of work per month. Founded in Johannesburg in 2014 and acquired by insurer Santam in May 2024 — institutional backing few marketplaces have.
cons: Lead quality varies by trade and region
vendors.typical_pricing: Pro pricing not published (sign up via Kandua for Pros).
vendors.za_specific_note: The Santam acquisition matters: Kandua is now wired into SA's biggest short-term insurer, and the vetting badge carries weight with homeowners. The first lead channel for most SA trades to try.

vendors.name: ProCompare
vendors.tagline: Pay-per-lead quote platform — leads from R40
vendors.market_position: Quote-comparison platform that sends each customer request to a maximum of six pros. Clean pay-per-lead economics — published lead prices, a self-set monthly budget, and no commission on the work.
cons: You pay for leads, not jobs
vendors.typical_pricing: Published: leads R40–R150 each; self-set monthly budget.
vendors.za_specific_note: One of the few SA lead platforms with transparent pricing — easy to test with a small budget and measure cost-per-job honestly.

vendors.name: Snupit
vendors.tagline: Large SA quote marketplace — 30,000+ monthly requests
vendors.market_position: A large SA marketplace claiming 350,000+ listed pros and 30,000+ monthly quote requests, on a pay-per-lead credit model with free lead notifications and no membership fee.
pros: No membership fee; free lead notifications
Credit model — pay only for leads you take
cons: Heavily contested leads at that scale
Credit pricing varies (not a flat rate)
vendors.typical_pricing: Pay-per-lead credits; notifications free; no membership fee.
vendors.za_specific_note: Worth running alongside Kandua/ProCompare to compare cost-per-won-job — the credit model makes a controlled trial cheap.

vendors.name: Uptasker
vendors.tagline: Free business directory + quote requests
vendors.market_position: An SA directory and quote platform for local experts across plumbing, electrical, cleaning and more — with free business listings, making it a zero-cost visibility channel.
pros: A free backlink/citation for your business
cons: Smaller audience than the big marketplaces
vendors.typical_pricing: Free listing; lead pricing not published.
vendors.za_specific_note: At minimum, claim the free listing — it costs nothing and adds a citation. Treat paid leads as a test, not a plan.

vendors.name: SimplePay
vendors.tagline: SA-native cloud payroll — EMP201, UIF & e@syFile built in
vendors.market_position: The default cloud payroll for SA small businesses — built locally around SARS compliance: automatic EMP201 monthly returns, UIF declarations, e@syFile integration and PAYE/UIF/SDL handling, with Xero and QuickBooks integrations.
pros: Auto EMP201 + UIF declarations, e@syFile integration
cons: Payroll only — no broader HR suite
Exact pricing via calculator (not a flat list)
Less suited to very large workforces
No field-service features (pairs with job software)
vendors.za_specific_note: The EMP201-within-7-days monthly rhythm is the SA payroll reality — SimplePay automates exactly that. The natural payroll layer under any of the job-management tools here.

vendors.name: PaySpace by Deel
vendors.tagline: "Built here, not adapted" SA payroll — now part of Deel
vendors.market_position: SA-built cloud payroll (now Deel's local-payroll arm) with automated PAYE, UIF, SDL and COIDA handling plus IRP5, EMP201, EMP501 and EEA reporting — strongest for growing businesses that want compliance depth.
pros: SA-origin engine with deep statutory coverage
cons: Assisted services billed extra (e.g. EMP501 help)
vendors.typical_pricing: Per-employee/month, quote-based; assisted EMP501 from R1,450/hr ex VAT.
vendors.za_specific_note: The step-up option when headcount and compliance complexity grow — biannual EMP501 reconciliation and COIDA tracking handled in one system.

vendors.name: Sage Payroll (SA)
vendors.tagline: Established small-business payroll with one-click SARS submissions
vendors.market_position: Sage's South African payroll for 1–99 employees — PAYE, UIF, SDL and IRP5 submissions to SARS "in a click", with the accountant familiarity of the Sage brand and published entry pricing.
cons: Tiered by employee count — costs step up
Less modern UI than the cloud-natives
Global product, SA is one edition
vendors.typical_pricing: Published: from R1,080/year (~R90/mo); tiers to 100 employees; 30-day trial.
vendors.za_specific_note: If your accountant already lives in Sage, this is the friction-free choice — and the published entry price is among the lowest real payroll options in SA.

vendors.name: Santam
vendors.tagline: SA’s largest short-term insurer — Contractors’ All Risks
vendors.market_position: South Africa's largest short-term insurer, with genuinely contractor-specific products: Contractors' All Risks, business liability with defective-workmanship extension, and construction professional indemnity — plus ownership of the Kandua marketplace.
cons: Premium brand can mean premium price
vendors.za_specific_note: Contractors' All Risks is the policy serious SA contractors actually need on site work — Santam is the reference market for it.

vendors.name: MiWay Business
vendors.tagline: Direct SME insurance — tools cover & broadform liability
vendors.market_position: Sanlam-group direct insurer with SME products that fit trades: all-risk tools and equipment cover away from premises, broadform liability including defective workmanship, and business-interruption cover — bought online without a broker.
pros: Direct online quotes — no broker needed
cons: Complex risks may still need a broker
vendors.za_specific_note: Tool theft is a constant SA trades risk — the away-from-premises all-risk cover is the clause to check first.

vendors.name: Old Mutual iWYZE Business
vendors.tagline: Direct SME cover that explicitly targets tradespeople
vendors.market_position: Old Mutual's direct insurer, whose business product explicitly names plumbers among its target customers — SME cover up to R30 million with 24/7 emergency assistance, bought direct.
pros: Cover up to R30M; 24/7 emergency assist
cons: Less specialist than broker-placed construction cover
Newer to business lines than Santam/Hollard
vendors.za_specific_note: One of the few SA insurers whose marketing speaks directly to tradespeople rather than generic "SMEs" — a sensible direct quote to benchmark.

vendors.name: King Price Business
vendors.tagline: Business cover incl. tools-of-trade & contractor niches
vendors.market_position: King Price's commercial lines cover tools of trade, public and employers' liability, and contractor/engineering niche covers — with the brand's value positioning, placed via brokers for commercial risks.
cons: Commercial lines via brokers (not instant online)
Smaller commercial book than the majors
vendors.za_specific_note: Worth a benchmark quote alongside MiWay/iWYZE — the tools-of-trade and liability combination is the core trades bundle.

vendors.name: Hollard
vendors.tagline: SME liability + construction & engineering via brokers
vendors.market_position: Major SA insurer with an SME all-in-one third-party liability policy and construction & engineering products placed through brokers — a serious alternative market for contractor risks.
vendors.za_specific_note: Ask your broker to run Hollard against Santam on Contractors' All Risks — competition between the two often sharpens the premium.

vendors.name: PIRB (Plumbing Industry Registration Board)
vendors.tagline: Plumber registration + the PIRB Certificate of Compliance
vendors.market_position: The SAQA-recognised professional body for SA plumbers. Registration is voluntary in law (there's no national plumber licence) — but PIRB CoCs are compulsory for geyser, heat-pump and solar-water-heater installations under SANS standards, and several municipal by-laws require registered plumbers.
pros: Issues the PIRB plumbing CoC (required for geysers/solar WH)
cons: Municipal by-law requirements vary by metro
vendors.za_specific_note: The accurate position: PIRB itself is voluntary, but the work that pays — geysers, heat pumps, solar water heaters — requires PIRB CoCs in practice. Software that tracks CoC numbers per job earns its keep here.

vendors.name: Dept of Employment & Labour (Electrical)
vendors.tagline: Statutory electrical-contractor registration + the CoC
vendors.market_position: The statutory regulator for electrical work: under the Electrical Installation Regulations 2009 (OHS Act), operating as an electrical contractor or certifying installations without DoEL registration is illegal. Registered persons (Single Phase Tester → Installation Electrician → Master) issue the Electrical Certificate of Compliance every installation — and every property transfer — requires.
pros: The legal gate for electrical contracting
CoCs required on every installation + property transfer
cons: Unregistered work is a criminal offence
CoC liability sits with the issuer
vendors.za_specific_note: The electrical CoC is SA's Gas-Safe-equivalent paperwork — legally loaded and constant. Job software that numbers, stores and reprints CoCs per installation is a genuinely local need.

vendors.name: SAQCC Gas
vendors.tagline: Statutory gas-practitioner registration
vendors.market_position: Registers gas practitioners on behalf of the DoEL under the Pressure Equipment Regulations: no person may install or remove a gas appliance, system or reticulation unless registered. Unregistered gas work is illegal — and typically voids the customer's home insurance.
pros: The legal requirement for all gas work
Gas CoCs protect customer insurance validity
cons: Category limits on what you may touch
vendors.za_specific_note: The insurance angle is the customer-facing sell: an unregistered gas install typically voids home cover — registered practitioners should say so in every quote.

vendors.name: CIDB (Construction Industry Development Board)
vendors.tagline: Contractor grading 1–9 — the gate to public-sector work
vendors.market_position: Maintains the statutory Register of Contractors, grading firms 1–9 by financial and works capability. Public clients may only award construction contracts to CIDB-registered contractors, and your grade caps the tender value you can bid (Grade 1 ≤ R500k up to Grade 9 unlimited).
pros: Required for ALL public-sector construction work
Grade signals capacity to private clients too
Clear upgrade path as your firm grows
cons: Grade caps the public work you can win
Upgrading needs proven financials + track record
Not needed for purely private work (confusing)
vendors.za_specific_note: SA's analogue to Gulf contractor classification: your CIDB grade is your ceiling on government tenders. Clean books (your accounting/payroll stack) are what move you up grades.

vendors.name: NHBRC (Home Builders Registration Council)
vendors.tagline: Mandatory registration + home enrolment for residential builders
vendors.market_position: Under the Housing Consumers Protection Measures Act, carrying on business as a home builder without NHBRC registration is a criminal offence — and every new home must be enrolled in the warranty fund before construction begins. A successor Act (25 of 2024) is signed but not yet in force.
pros: The legal gate for residential building
cons: New 2024 Act will tighten rules further (~2027)
vendors.za_specific_note: If you build homes, NHBRC registration and pre-construction enrolment are non-negotiable — build the 15-day enrolment lead time into every project plan.

vendors.name: SARS (VAT, PAYE & eFiling)
vendors.tagline: 15% VAT, EMP201 payroll returns — and e-invoicing coming
vendors.market_position: The tax authority: 15% VAT (compulsory registration above R2.3m turnover from April 2026 — recently tripled from R1m), monthly EMP201 (PAYE+UIF+SDL) within 7 days of month-end, all via eFiling/e@syFile. E-invoicing is NOT yet mandatory — but SARS has confirmed a Peppol-based model phasing in around 2026–2028, large taxpayers first.
pros: E-invoicing framework announced with lead time
cons: EMP201 due within 7 days of month-end — relentless
E-invoicing mandate will force software change (~2026–28)
vendors.za_specific_note: Two software implications: payroll must automate EMP201 (SimplePay/PaySpace/Sage do), and "Peppol-ready" invoicing is a forward-looking buying criterion as SARS phases in e-invoicing.

vendors.name: COIDA / Compensation Fund
vendors.tagline: Workers’ comp + the Letter of Good Standing sites demand
vendors.market_position: All employers with one or more employees must register with the Compensation Fund and pay annual assessments. Under the Construction Regulations, clients must verify a principal contractor is in good standing before work starts — making the annually renewed Letter of Good Standing a de-facto prerequisite for site access and tenders.
pros: Covers employees for injuries on duty
Letter of Good Standing unlocks sites + tenders
cons: Annual renewal — expiry locks you out of sites
vendors.za_specific_note: Track the Letter of Good Standing expiry like a licence — an expired letter can stop a project mid-stream. Document-expiry tracking is a real SA software feature need.
